entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: An important source of dietary protein to over a billion people globally.

entities.name: SEED PROTEIN CONTENT
entities.description: A trait in pigeonpea that has received limited attention for genetic enhancement.

entities.name: GENOMICS-ASSISTED BREEDING
entities.description: A breeding approach to accelerate genetic gain for seed protein content.

entities.name: WHOLE GENOME RE-SEQUENCING
entities.description: A technique used to identify sequence-based markers and candidate genes for seed protein content.

entities.name: SEQUENCE-BASED MARKERS
entities.description: Markers identified using WGRS data to be associated with seed protein content.

entities.name: CANDIDATE GENES
entities.description: Genes identified through WGRS and variant filtering associated with seed protein content.

entities.name: COMMON VARIANT FILTERING STRATEGY
entities.description: A strategy used on WGRS data combined with gene function knowledge to identify sequence variants.

entities.name: SANGER SEQUENCING
entities.description: A method used to confirm the presence of sequence variants in genes.

entities.name: CAPS/DCAPS MARKERS
entities.description: Markers converted from sequence variants used to identify polymorphism between low and high seed protein content genotypes.

entities.name: F2 POPULATION
entities.description: A population resulting from the cross of ICP 5529 and ICP 11605 used to assay markers.

entities.name: ICP 5529
entities.description: A pigeonpea genotype with high seed protein content.

entities.name: ICP 11605
entities.description: A pigeonpea genotype with low seed protein content.

entities.name: GO MOLECULAR FUNCTION CATEGORIES
entities.description: Categories to which the identified genes were assigned.
